title: Phenomenon 40
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:"Pitstops"}}
The man scoffed, he didn't even have the ham for his sandwich. He snickered, poking fun at his own memory. "Any day now I'll lose my head," the young man jokingly thought. He continued over to his pantry, until he noticed an unnatural force pulling him down. As he looked downward, it almost looked as if his foot was phasing through the flooring. He screamed, pulling his foot backward - but it only sent him further down into the spiral. It was like a wormhole, he slowly and painfully phased through the floorboards until it was just his head poking out. The rest of his body felt cold and senseless. He flailed his body about though he still couldn't feel anything. This movement somehow loosened the floorboards' grip on his head, almost fully falling in. With only his eyes poking out, he tried to scream but nothing would come out. Or maybe something did, maybe the sound was just in another dimension. He eventually fell fully into this wormhole. He had his eyes shut, praying this wouldn't be his end. And his prayers were answered. He felt something; something soft, something damp. He heard something, an ear-piercing buzz that wouldn't seem to stop. He opened his eyes and saw everything. He was in a room saturated in yellow, not a singular object was spared from the sickly hue. The room was incoherently designed and almost looked alien, though there was a strange familiarity to it. He looked up to be blinded by the origin of that putrid hum. Fluorescent lighting dotted the roof of this strange place this man had found himself in. The man was utterly confused and had a strange feeling he couldn't put his finger on. It was a strange sense of familiarity, déjà vu even. He maniacally laughed, it was just a dream. He'd wake up in his bed right... '''NOW!''' By a stroke of luck, his timing was impeccable. He noticed that strange, tugging feeling in his foot again. As he looked down, he saw that he was being sucked into the floor; just like back home. As everyone would be, he was initially quite surprised, attempting to pull his foot back until he realized what was happening. As he tugged his leg backward, more of his body was enveloped in the carpet. The body parts sucked into the floor were cold and numb, with no sensations at all. He descended into another reality, another dimension. A dimension he calls home. [[The Frontrooms]]. When he came to, he was on the hard floor and drenched in sweat. He got to his feet, and he noticed his sandwich was already made for him with a note beside it. {{Collapse|num=1|initial=
"Come back soon!"
|final=
"Come back soon!"
|body= This wasn't normal, he was smart enough to know that. He had to inform everyone about this. He had this compelling urge to tell everyone about this, so that's exactly what he did. He devoured his sandwich before quickly heading upstairs to his laptop. He sent an email to every singular journalist and popular news program he knew. He became obsessed with this phenomenon and dove deeper. He couldn't find anything anywhere. It was like no one had ever talked about it before, that was until he found it. [[05-12-19|A 4chan post]]. The image looked exactly like what he had seen in that world, and it gave a name for it. The Backrooms. He had to spread the knowledge, he just ''had'' to! So, of course, he got to writing. It was a lousy Reddit post, one that could easily be mistaken as a creepypasta, but he had to get the knowledge out in some form. But that's when it happened. Right before he was going to post it, he felt the tugging feeling in his back. His heart sank and he froze. He couldn't move as his body slowly phased through his cheap, synthetic leather chair. It was the exact same feeling he felt a few hours ago, the parts of his body that escaped his current realm were motionless and deprived of sensory input. He screamed at the top of the lungs, though no one came to his aid. The chair enveloped him, and he woke up in that same world he found himself in just hours ago. His stay was now permanent as he surveyed his surroundings. He had entered the Lobby, and this time there's no way out. He had reached a dead end, forced to permanently wander these putrid spaces for the rest of his life. His attempts to spread information on the Backrooms were futile, as his emails all went to the spam folder and his post was never published. He just couldn't keep his mouth shut. }}

{{Collapse|num=2|initial=> OPEN DOCUMENTATION|final=> CLOSE DOCUMENTATION|body= '''Pitstops''', classified as Phenomenon 40, is a phenomenon that - while connected to the Backrooms - only occurs in [[The Frontrooms]]. It is described by its victims to start with a slight tugging to a random part of their body that has contact to a physical object. If one were to look down following this tugging feeling - which is a basic human instinct - they would notice that whatever part of their body that was feeling the tugging would seemingly be phasing through whatever object it was touching. As the affected person notices this, they will have the temptation to pull the affected limb back. If they do so, the rest of their body will slowly be pulled into the object until they have fully phased into the Backrooms. It should be noted that, if one is able to resist the temptation, the process will still activate though will be much slower and intensify the pain felt in the regular process. After the person falls into the Backrooms, they will be unconscious for around an hour before awakening, where they will then be given 20-30 minutes to take in their surroundings. Once the time frame is up, they would undergo the previous process though the affected person would instead be sent to [[The Frontrooms]]. Following their awakening in the Frontrooms, they will find themselves laying on the floor drenched in sweat. In most cases of Phenomenon 40, a note will be found somewhere near the affected person's awakening site that would typically say "Come back soon!" or some other variation of the sentence. Affected persons, following their awakening in the Frontrooms, will feel an inescapable urge to investigate the realm they had just fallen into further. Typically, affected persons will contact news stations and journalists to describe what they had just experienced and will scour the internet for any mentions of the world they had fallen into, or at least something similar. The affected person will become irrationally obsessed with investigating the Backrooms. The process of Phenomenon 40 typically ends when an affected person is about to spread information relating to the Backrooms on television or a popular website. Right before they are about to talk about or post their experience they will feel that familiar tugging feeling once more. They will freeze up, unable to move a muscle as they slowly phase through whatever object they were touching beforehand and are finally condemned to the Backrooms. Any footage relating to the now wanderer will be completely erased, though memories of their existence will remain present. It is believed, however, that if an affected person manages to fight the urge to spread information relating to the Backrooms they will not be sent back. Able to live out the rest of their life with the knowledge of the Backrooms. This, of course, cannot be proven due to the [[The Database|Database's]] reliance on purely Backrooms-circulated information. }}
---- {{Collapse|initial=> Open Author and Licensing Information|final=> Close Author and Licensing Information|body=Written by [[User:Fungrly Scrunkly|the fungus]],
